title: fix: emit tool-result/tool-error chunks incrementally as each tool completes

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: feat: stream tool arguments incrementally across all tool renderers
similarOrDuplicatePRs.relevance: Related but different scope — that PR streamed tool *arguments* incrementally, this one streams tool *results* incrementally

summary: When multiple tools run in parallel via the `foreach` handler, all tool-result/tool-error chunks are batched and emitted only after ALL tools finish (because `Promise.all` blocks). This PR moves chunk emission from `llmMappingStep` into `toolCallStep` so each tool emits its result/error chunk immediately upon completion. Changes span 4 files: (1) `tool-call-step.ts` accepts an optional `ToolChunkEmitter` callback and emits tool-result/tool-error chunks as each tool completes, (2) `llm-mapping-step.ts` accepts a `chunksAlreadyEmitted` flag to skip duplicate emission, (3) `index.ts` wires the chunkEmitter through ProcessorRunner + safeEnqueue, (4) `mastracode/src/index.ts` adds an unrelated `memory` config override (should be in a separate PR). Author addressed all 3 CodeRabbit review issues in a follow-up commit.
maintainerNotes: Well-structured community contribution with clear problem description and a sound approach. Key concerns: (1) **Merge conflicts** — author needs to rebase onto latest main. (2) **No changeset** — changeset-bot flagged this; a changeset for `@mastra/core` is needed. (3) **No new tests** — author states existing 31 tests pass, but no new test verifying incremental emission behavior was added. (4) **Unrelated change** — the `mastracode/src/index.ts` change (adding a `memory` config override) is unrelated to the tool streaming fix and should be split into a separate PR. (5) **CI checks still pending** — core tests, memory tests, MCP tests, E2E tests, etc. are gated behind secrets and haven't run yet (external contributor). (6) CodeRabbit raised a valid nitpick about using `safeEnqueue` for `tool-call-approval`/`tool-call-suspended` chunks that wasn't addressed.
